diagrams.title: T-Distribution vs Normal Distribution Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows how t-distribution has heavier tails and is more spread out than the standard normal distribution
elements: T-curve diverges more at tails for smaller df
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why t-distribution accounts for uncertainty in small samples

diagrams.title: Critical Regions in T-Test
diagrams.description: Shows shaded critical regions for one-tailed and two-tailed t-tests
elements: Two-tailed: 2.5% in each tail for α=0.05
diagrams.purpose: Understand decision-making regions in hypothesis testing

diagrams.title: Paired vs Independent Samples Design
diagrams.description: Shows experimental designs for different types of t-tests
elements: Paired: Same subjects measured twice with connecting lines
Independent: Two separate groups measured once
Labels showing n pairs or n₁, n₂ for each group
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between different t-test scenarios and when to apply each
